conflict_type: Conflicts between Keyword Constraints and Phrase Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a thank you email to a professor after a successful internship program.
org_constraint: The phrase "valuable experience" must appear exactly twice in the email.
new_constraint: The keyword "experience" must be used exclusively as a verb.
instruction: Write a thank you email to a professor after a successful internship program. The phrase "valuable experience" must appear exactly twice in the email. The keyword "experience" must be used exclusively as a verb.
response:
Subject: Thank You - Reflecting on My Internship with [Professor's Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

As my internship with you concludes, I wanted to express my sincere gratitude for this incredible opportunity. My time in your lab/under your guidance has been tremendously rewarding.

The knowledge and skills I've gained are truly exceptional. I particularly appreciated [mention a specific project, skill learned, or piece of advice]. It provided me with a very **valuable experience** in [specific field/skill]. I feel I have significantly enhanced my ability to [mention a related skill] during this period.

I am also so grateful for your mentorship. Your willingness to guide me and share your expertise made this journey exceptionally enriching. I believe this whole process allowed me to really **experience** the real-world application of what I've learned in class, providing a second, incredibly **valuable experience** that will shape my future academic and professional path.

Thank you once again for investing your time and energy in my development. I hope to stay in touch and look forward to potentially contributing to your work again in the future.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID/Contact Information]<turn|>
